title: Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken

seo.title: Easy Chicken Cacciatore Recipe (Pollo alla Cacciatora)
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with our easy hunter-style chicken recipe. Perfect for a hearty family meal!
seo.h1: Authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora)
h2Headings: What is Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora)?
Tips for the Best Pollo alla Cacciatora
seo.imageAlt: A rustic plate of authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of Italy with this hearty and flavorful Chicken Cacciatore, cooked hunter-style.

description: Pollo alla Cacciatora is a rustic and beloved Italian classic, featuring tender chicken braised in a rich, savory sauce with tomatoes, bell peppers, onions, and often mushrooms. This hunter-style dish embodies the warmth and robust flavors of traditional Italian home cooking, perfect for a comforting family meal. The slow simmering allows the ingredients to meld beautifully, creating a deeply satisfying and aromatic experience.

ingredients.notes: bone-in, skin-on, thighs and drumsticks preferred
such as Chianti or Merlot, optional

instructions.title: Prepare and Season the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat chicken pieces dry with paper towels. Season generously all over with salt and freshly ground black pepper. This helps create a flavorful crust.

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add chicken pieces, skin-side down, and sear until golden brown and crispy on all sides, about 5-7 minutes per side. Remove the browned chicken from the pot and set it aside on a plate.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Vegetables
instructions.text: Reduce heat to medium. Add the chopped onion to the same pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent, about 5 minutes. Add the minced garlic, sliced red and green bell peppers, and sliced mushrooms. Sauté for another 7-10 minutes, until the vegetables are tender-crisp.

instructions.title: Deglaze and Build the Sauce
instructions.text: If using, pour in the dry red wine and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon. Let it simmer for 2 minutes until slightly reduced. Stir in the crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, dried oregano, and the sprigs of fresh rosemary and thyme. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.

instructions.title: Simmer the Cacciatora
instructions.text: Return the browned chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the sauce. Ensure the chicken is mostly submerged. Cover the pot, reduce the heat to low, and let it simmer for 30-35 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through, tender, and the sauce has thickened slightly. The internal temperature of the chicken should reach 165F (74C).

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Carefully remove and discard the rosemary and thyme sprigs from the pot. Taste the sauce and adjust seasoning with more salt and pepper if necessary. Ladle the Pollo alla Cacciatora onto plates, garnishing generously with fresh chopped parsley before serving hot. It pairs wonderfully with crusty bread, polenta, or pasta.

tips: For truly tender chicken, consider using bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces as they impart more flavor and moisture during the slow simmering process.
Do not skip the browning step for the chicken; it adds a crucial layer of flavor and helps create a rich depth in the final sauce.
If you prefer a thicker sauce, uncover the pot for the last 10-15 minutes of simmering, or remove the chicken and reduce the sauce further over medium-high heat.
article:
Pollo alla Cacciatora, or Hunter Style Chicken, is a dish that embodies the rustic charm and comforting flavors of Italian cuisine. This classic stew, hailing from the heart of Italy, translates literally to "hunter's style chicken," a nod to its humble origins where hunters would prepare their catch with readily available ingredients like tomatoes, onions, mushrooms, and herbs. It's a dish that has graced countless family tables for generations, cherished for its rich, savory sauce and succulent, tender chicken. If you're looking to bring a taste of authentic Italy into your kitchen, mastering Pollo alla Cacciatora is a delightful journey.

## What is Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora)?

At its heart, Chicken Cacciatore is a testament to simple, wholesome ingredients transformed into something extraordinary through slow cooking. While the exact recipe can vary significantly from region to region within Italy—some favoring red wine, others white, some including bell peppers, others not—the core concept remains the same: chicken braised in a savory, aromatic sauce.

Typically, the dish features bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces (often thighs and drumsticks) that are browned to perfection, then simmered in a rich tomato-based sauce. This sauce is usually built upon a sofrito of onions and garlic, often enhanced with bell peppers, mushrooms, and a fragrant bouquet of herbs like rosemary and thyme. The slow cooking process allows the flavors to meld beautifully, resulting in chicken that's fall-off-the-bone tender and a deeply flavorful sauce perfect for soaking up with crusty bread or serving over pasta. It's a hearty, satisfying meal that speaks volumes of Italian culinary tradition.

## Ingredients for Hunter Style Chicken

While the specific ingredients for Chicken Cacciatore can vary regionally, a classic and universally loved version typically calls for:

*   **Chicken:** 2-3 pounds bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces (thighs, drumsticks, or a cut-up whole chicken). Bone-in adds flavor, and skin-on helps keep the meat moist.
*   **Olive Oil:** 2-3 tablespoons, for browning the chicken and sautéing vegetables.
*   **Onion:** 1 large yellow onion, chopped.
*   **Garlic:** 4-6 cloves, minced.
*   **Bell Peppers:** 2-3 bell peppers (red, yellow, or orange for color and sweetness), deseeded and sliced.
*   **Mushrooms:** 8 ounces cremini or white button mushrooms, sliced.
*   **Tomatoes:** 1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes or diced tomatoes, or 1 (24-ounce) jar tomato passata.
*   **Wine:** 1/2 cup dry red or white wine (Chianti, Pinot Grigio, or a simple table wine are good choices).
*   **Chicken Broth:** 1/2 cup to 1 cup, as needed.
*   **Herbs:** 2 sprigs fresh rosemary, 3 sprigs fresh thyme, 1-2 bay leaves. Fresh parsley for garnish.
*   **Seasoning:** Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.
*   **Optional:** A few slices of pancetta or bacon, rendered before adding other ingredients, for an extra layer of savory depth.

## Step-by-Step Chicken Cacciatore Instructions

Making a truly authentic Chicken Cacciatore is a rewarding culinary journey that results in an incredibly flavorful dish. Here’s how to do it:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towels. Season generously with salt and black pepper. If using, render the pancetta or bacon in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat until crispy. Remove the pancetta/bacon and set aside, leaving the rendered fat in the pot.
2.  **Brown the Chicken:** Add olive oil to the pot (if not using pancetta fat) and heat over medium-high heat. Place the chicken skin-side down in the hot oil (in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding) and sear for 5-7 minutes until the skin is deeply golden brown and crispy. Flip and sear the other side for 3-5 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside.
3.  **Sauté Aromatics:** Reduce the heat to medium. Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the bell peppers and cook for another 5 minutes until slightly tender. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
4.  **Add Mushrooms and Deglaze:** Add the sliced mushrooms to the pot and cook, stirring frequently, until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 5-7 minutes. Pour in the wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon. Let the wine simmer for 2-3 minutes until it has mostly evaporated.
5.  **Build the Sauce:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or diced tomatoes/passata), chicken broth, rosemary sprigs, thyme sprigs, and bay leaves. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.
6.  **Simmer the Chicken:** Return the browned chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the sauce. Ensure the chicken is partially submerged. Cover the pot, reduce the heat to low, and simmer for 45-60 minutes, or until the chicken is fork-tender and cooked through.
7.  **Finish and Serve:** Remove the lid and let the sauce simmer uncovered for another 10-15 minutes to thicken slightly, if desired. Remove the herb sprigs and bay leaves. Taste the sauce and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley and the reserved crispy pancetta/bacon, if using.

## Tips for the Best Pollo alla Cacciatora

Achieving the perfect Chicken Cacciatore relies on a few key techniques and considerations:

*   **Don't Skip Browning the Chicken:** This step is crucial for developing deep flavor and color. The Maillard reaction creates delicious crusty bits that contribute immensely to the overall taste of the dish.
*   **Bone-in, Skin-on Chicken:** While boneless, skinless chicken can be used, bone-in pieces impart more flavor to the sauce and tend to stay juicier during the long simmer. The skin, once browned, also adds texture.
*   **Quality Ingredients:** Since this dish relies on relatively few ingredients, the quality of each matters. Use good quality canned tomatoes and fresh, vibrant herbs for the best flavor.
*   **Slow and Low:** Patience is key. The long, slow simmer is what makes the chicken incredibly tender and allows all the flavors to meld beautifully into a cohesive, rich sauce.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pot:** When browning the chicken or sautéing vegetables, work in batches if your pot isn't large enough. Overcrowding steams the ingredients instead of browning them, preventing flavor development.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Always taste your sauce before serving. Adjust salt, pepper, or even a pinch of sugar if your tomatoes are particularly acidic.

## Serving Suggestions for Chicken Cacciatore

The rich, savory flavors of Chicken Cacciatore pair wonderfully with a variety of sides, making it a versatile meal for any occasion.

*   **Pasta:** Serve it over wide, flat pasta like tagliatelle, pappardelle, or fettuccine, allowing the noodles to soak up every drop of the delicious sauce.
*   **Polenta:** Creamy polenta is a classic accompaniment, providing a comforting base that beautifully complements the robust flavors of the chicken and sauce.
*   **Crusty Bread:** A loaf of warm, crusty Italian bread is essential for mopping up every last bit of the rich sauce.
*   **Rice or Mashed Potatoes:** For a simpler side, fluffy white rice or creamy mashed potatoes are excellent choices.
*   **Vegetables:** A light green salad or some simply steamed green beans can offer a fresh contrast to the hearty stew.
*   **Wine Pairing:** A medium-bodied red wine like Chianti, Merlot, or a Sangiovese will complement the tomato and herb notes of the dish perfectly. If you used white wine in the recipe, a crisp, dry white would also be a good choice.

## Variations and Substitutions

Beyond the classic preparation, there are many ways to adapt Chicken Cacciatore to your taste or what you have on hand:

*   **Vegetable Additions:** Feel free to include other vegetables like carrots, celery, zucchini, or even olives and capers for a saltier, tangier kick.
*   **Herbs:** While rosemary and thyme are traditional, you can also add oregano, basil, or a bay leaf. A pinch of red pepper flakes can provide a subtle warmth.
*   **Wine Alternatives:** If you prefer not to use wine, you can substitute it with an equal amount of chicken broth or a splash of balsamic vinegar for acidity.
*   **Different Meats:** While traditionally chicken, the "cacciatora" style can be applied to other meats like rabbit, pork, or even firm white fish, though the cooking times would need adjustment.
*   **Gluten-Free and Dairy-Free:** Pollo alla Cacciatora is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free, making it an excellent option for those with dietary restrictions. Just ensure your broth and other ingredients don't contain hidden additives.
*   **Slow Cooker/Instant Pot:** This dish adapts beautifully to a slow cooker or Instant Pot for hands-off cooking. Brown the chicken and sauté the aromatics on the stovetop first for best flavor, then combine everything in your appliance.

Pollo alla Cacciatora is more than just a meal; it's an experience, a taste of Italian heritage that brings warmth and satisfaction to any table. Its robust flavors and comforting nature make it a perfect dish for family dinners or entertaining friends. Don't be intimidated by the seemingly long cooking time; the hands-on effort is minimal, and the reward is a truly unforgettable culinary delight. Give this Hunter Style Chicken a try, and discover why it remains a beloved classic.
